children.heading: Export-Import Bank assistance for exports to sanctioned persons
children.label: 1 Export-Import Bank assistance for exports to sanctioned persons
children.text: The President may direct the Export-Import Bank of the United States not to give approval to the issuance of any guarantee, insurance, extension of credit, or participation in the extension of credit in connection with the export of any goods or services to the sanctioned person.

children.label: A
children.text: the Export Administration Act of 1979 ( et seq.) (as continued in effect pursuant to the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.));50 U.S.C. 460150 U.S.C. 1701

children.label: B
children.text: the Arms Export Control Act ( et seq.);22 U.S.C. 2751

children.label: C
children.text: the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 ( et seq.); or42 U.S.C. 2011

children.label: D
children.text: any other statute that requires the prior review and approval of the United States Government as a condition for the export or reexport of goods or services.

children.heading: Loans from United States financial institutions
children.label: 3 Loans from United States financial institutions
children.text: The President may prohibit any United States financial institution from making loans or providing credits to the sanctioned person totaling more than $10,000,000 in any 12-month period unless the person is engaged in activities to relieve human suffering and the loans or credits are provided for such activities.

children.heading: Loans from international financial institutions
children.label: 4 Loans from international financial institutions
children.text: The President may direct the United States executive director to each international financial institution to use the voice and vote of the United States to oppose any loan from the international financial institution that would benefit the sanctioned person.

children.heading: Prohibition on designation as primary dealer
children.label: A Prohibition on designation as primary dealer
children.text: Neither the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System nor the Federal Reserve Bank of New York may designate, or permit the continuation of any prior designation of, the financial institution as a primary dealer in United States Government debt instruments.

children.heading: Prohibition on service as a repository of Government funds
children.label: B Prohibition on service as a repository of Government funds
children.text: The financial institution may not serve as agent of the United States Government or serve as repository for United States Government funds.

children.heading: Procurement sanction
children.label: 6 Procurement sanction
children.text: The United States Government may not procure, or enter into any contract for the procurement of, any goods or services from the sanctioned person.

children.heading: Foreign exchange
children.label: 7 Foreign exchange
children.text: The President may, pursuant to such regulations as the President may prescribe, prohibit any transactions in foreign exchange that are subject to the jurisdiction of the United States and in which the sanctioned person has any interest.

children.heading: Banking transactions
children.label: 8 Banking transactions
children.text: The President may, pursuant to such regulations as the President may prescribe, prohibit any transfers of credit or payments between financial institutions or by, through, or to any financial institution, to the extent that such transfers or payments are subject to the jurisdiction of the United States and involve any interest of the sanctioned person.

children.label: A
children.text: acquiring, holding, withholding, using, transferring, withdrawing, transporting, importing, or exporting any property that is subject to the jurisdiction of the United States and with respect to which the sanctioned person has any interest;

children.label: B
children.text: dealing in or exercising any right, power, or privilege with respect to such property; or

children.label: C
children.text: conducting any transaction involving such property.

children.heading: Ban on investment in equity or debt of sanctioned person
children.label: 10 Ban on investment in equity or debt of sanctioned person
children.text: The President may, pursuant to such regulations or guidelines as the President may prescribe, prohibit any United States person from investing in or purchasing significant amounts of equity or debt instruments of the sanctioned person.

children.heading: Exclusion of corporate officers
children.label: 11 Exclusion of corporate officers
children.text: The President may direct the Secretary of State to deny a visa to, and the Secretary of Homeland Security to exclude from the United States, any alien that the President determines is a corporate officer or principal of, or a shareholder with a controlling interest in, the sanctioned person.

children.heading: Sanctions on principal executive officers
children.label: 12 Sanctions on principal executive officers
children.text: The President may impose on the principal executive officer or officers of the sanctioned person, or on persons performing similar functions and with similar authorities as such officer or officers, any of the sanctions under this subsection.

children.heading: Sanctioned person defined
children.label: b Sanctioned person defined
children.text: In this section, the term “sanctioned person” means a person subject to sanctions under section 9524(a)(2), 9525(b), 9526(a), or 9527(a) of this title.
